channel.title: Sylow-Theorems on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Sylow-Theorems on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Sylow Theorems: Dissecting Finite Groups
item.description: The structure theory of finite groups rests on one fundamental observation: the prime factorization of $|G|$ constrains what subgroups $G$ can have. Lagrange’s theorem tells us that the order of any subgroup divides $|G|$ , but the converse is false in general — not every divisor of $|G|$ corresponds to a subgroup. The Sylow theorems dramatically sharpen the picture: for each prime power dividing $|G|$ , a subgroup of that order not only exists but has strong structural properties. These results, proved by Ludwig Sylow in 1872, remain the single most effective tool for analyzing finite groups.
